item.title: Addressing Heterogeneity in Federated Learning: Challenges and Solutions for a Shared Production Environment
item.description: Citation: T. Legler, V. Hegiste, A. Anwar, M. Ruskowski. “Addressing Heterogeneity in Federated Learning: Challenges and Solutions for a Shared Production Environment.” In: Procedia Computer Science, Vol. 253, 6th International Conference on Industry 4.0 and Smart Manufacturing. Elsevier, 2025, pp. 2831–2840.

item.title: Benchmarking Federated Learning Under Realistic Non-IID Conditions: A Structured Partitioning Approach Using ImageNet
item.description: Citation: T. Legler, V. Hegiste, M. Ruskowski. “Benchmarking Federated Learning Under Realistic Non-IID Conditions: A Structured Partitioning Approach Using ImageNet.” In: Proceedings of the 2025 IEEE 45th International Conference on Distributed Computing Systems Workshops (ICDCSW). IEEE, 2025, pp. —. DOI: 10.1109/ICDCSW63273.2025.00137.

item.title: Contrastive Left-Right Wearable Sensors (IMUs) Consistency Matching for HAR
item.description: Citation: Nshimyimana, Dominique, Vitor Fortes Rey, and Paul Lukowicz. “Contrastive left-right wearable sensors (imus) consistency matching for har.” Activity, Behavior, and Healthcare Computing. CRC Press, 2025. 88-104.

item.title: CPPM Copilot: Proposing an AI-Based Assistant for Manual Assembly Tasks in a Flexible Production
item.description: Citation: J. Nussbaum, T. Legler, M. Ruskowski,. “CPPM Copilot: Proposing an AI-Based Assistant for Manual Assembly Tasks in a Flexible Production”. In: Advances in Artificial Intelligence in Manufacturing II. Springer, Cham., 2025, pp. 80–88

item.title: eFAirWrite: Bringing energy efficient text entry to next generation smart devices
item.description: Citation: eFAirWrite: Bringing Energy Efficient Text Entry to Next Generation Smart Devices. M. M. Ghaffar, A. Abdullah, J. Younas, V. Rybalkin, J. Ney, P. Lukowiczc, N. Wehn. Expert Systems With Applications, Elsevier, 2025. https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0957417425019256?via%3Dihub

item.title: Embedded Inter-Subject Variability in Adversarial Learning for Inertial Sensor-Based Human Activity Recognition
item.description: Citation: F. M. Calatrava-Nicolás, S. Miyauchi, V. F. Rey, P. Lukowicz, T. Stoyanov and O. M. Mozos, “Embedded Inter-Subject Variability in Adversarial Learning for Inertial Sensor-Based Human Activity Recognition,” 2025 IEEE 35th International Workshop on Machine Learning for Signal Processing (MLSP), Istanbul, Turkiye, 2025, pp. 1-6, doi: 10.1109/MLSP62443.2025.11204225.

item.title: Ethische Implikationen von Embedded AI: zur Ambiguität von care and control

item.description: Citation: V. Hegiste, V. Goyal, T. Legler, M. Ruskowski. “Federated Action Recognition for Smart Worker Assistance Using FastPose.” In: Proceedings of the 2025 3rd International Conference on Federated Learning Technologies and Applications (FLTA). IEEE, 2025. DOI: 10.1109/FLTA67013.2025.11336524.

Ruskowski, “Federated Residual Reinforcement Learning for Collaborative Robot Skill Learning in Industry,” 2025 3rd International Conference on Federated Learning Technologies and Applications (FLTA), Dubrovnik, Croatia, 2025, pp. 530-536, doi: 10.1109/FLTA67013.2025.11336679.

item.title: Inertial Sensor-Based Hierarchical Contrastive Multi-View Learning for Human Activity Recognition
item.description: Citation: Calatrava-Nicolas, F. M., Rey, V. F., Lukowicz, P., & Mozos, O. M. (2025, October). Inertial Sensor-Based Hierarchical Contrastive Multi-View Learning for Human Activity Recognition. In Proceedings of the 2025 ACM International Symposium on Wearable Computers (pp. 106-112).

item.title: PACL+: Online continual learning using proxy-anchor and contrastive loss with Gaussian replay for sensor-based human activity recognition
item.description: Citation: Mittal, D. A., Rey, V. F., Bello, H., Lukowicz, P., & Suh, S. (2025). PACL+: Online continual learning using proxy-anchor and contrastive loss with Gaussian replay for sensor-based human activity recognition. Expert Systems with Applications, 292, 128603.

item.title: PIM: Physics-Informed Multi-task Pre-training for Improving Inertial Sensor-Based Human Activity Recognition
item.description: Citation: D. Nshimyimana, V. F. Rey, S. Suh, B. Zhou and P. Lukowicz, “PIM: Physics-Informed Multi-task Pre-training for Improving Inertial Sensor-Based Human Activity Recognition,” 2025 International Conference on Activity and Behavior Computing (ABC), Al Ain, United Arab Emirates, 2025, pp. 1-11, doi: 10.1109/ABC64332.2025.11118365. keywords: {Accuracy;Training data;Self-supervised learning;Benchmark testing;Multitasking;Data augmentation;Mathematical models;Data models;Human activity recognition;Wearable sensors;Human activity recognition;Self-supervised learning;Physics-inspired neural networks;Wearable sensors;Pretext tasks},
